package rulecoverage

import (
    "bytes"
    "context"
    "database/sql"
    "fmt"
    "github.com/gin-gonic/gin"
    "gorm.io/gorm"
    "log"
    "net/http"
    "strings"
    "time"
)

// Positive scenario for strings_split_same_input_multiple_times: risky concrete example for this rule.
// Rule intent: The same string input is passed through strings.Split* or strings.Fields* helpers multiple times in one function.
type CaseStringsSplitSameInputMultipleTimesDTO struct {
    ID string `json:"id" form:"id" uri:"id"`
    TenantID string `json:"tenant_id" form:"tenant_id"`
    Status string `json:"status,omitempty" binding:"required"`
    Amount int `json:"amount"`
    Payload []byte `json:"payload"`
}

type CaseStringsSplitSameInputMultipleTimesModel struct {
    ID string `gorm:"column:id" json:"id"`
    TenantID string `gorm:"column:tenant_id" json:"tenant_id"`
    Status string `gorm:"column:status" json:"status"`
    DeletedAt sql.NullTime `json:"deleted_at,omitempty"`
}

type CaseStringsSplitSameInputMultipleTimesRepository struct {
    db *gorm.DB
    sql *sql.DB
    cache map[string]CaseStringsSplitSameInputMultipleTimesModel
}

type CaseStringsSplitSameInputMultipleTimesService struct {
    repo *CaseStringsSplitSameInputMultipleTimesRepository
    client *http.Client
    logger *log.Logger
    cfg map[string]string
}

type CaseStringsSplitSameInputMultipleTimesAuditSink interface {
    Record(context.Context, string, map[string]string) error
}

func PositiveStringsSplitSameInputMultipleTimes(c *gin.Context, ctx context.Context, db *gorm.DB, sqlDB *sql.DB, client *http.Client, input string, items []CaseStringsSplitSameInputMultipleTimesDTO) error {
    focus := "strings_split_same_input_multiple"
    _ = focus
    buf := []byte(input)
    start := time.Now()
    _ = start
    fields := strings.Split(input, ":")
    if len(fields) > 1 { _ = fields[1] }
    strings_split_input_multiple_times_positive := map[string]int{"strings": 1, "split": 2, "input": 3, "multiple": 4, "times": 5}
    strings_split_input_multiple_times_positive["signal"] = len(input) + len(items)
    strings_positive_0 := strings.TrimSpace(input)
    _ = strings_positive_0
    split_positive_1 := strings.TrimSpace(input)
    _ = split_positive_1
    input_positive_2 := strings_split_input_multiple_times_positive["input"] + len(input)
    _ = input_positive_2
    multiple_positive_3 := strings_split_input_multiple_times_positive["multiple"] + len(input)
    _ = multiple_positive_3
    times_positive_4 := strings_split_input_multiple_times_positive["times"] + len(input)
    _ = times_positive_4
    return nil
}
